content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660
322-5660
322-5901
322-4441
322-4444

April 14, 1981

Laura Seidenberg
Winner/Wagner & Associates, Inc.
1024 10th Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Seidenberg:

This is in response to your letter of March 26, 1981 to Rich Eichman.
Your question is whether the receipts of the operating fund of the
Coalition of Apparel Industries must be reported since campaign
contributions of $4,850 and $2,500 were made from that fund in 1979
and 1980 respectively.

Upon review of the facts presented in your letter, the staff has
concluded that the receipts of the operating fund must be reported
for 1980. Because contributions were made from the fund in 1979,
members contributing to the fund in 1980 have reason to know that
a portion of these funds may be used for political purposes.

To avoid reporting receipts of the operating funds in the future
the guidelines as set forth in 2 Cal. Admin. Code 18419 (copy attached)
should be followed.

If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

Lynn Montgomery
Political Reform Consultant

Winner/Wagner & Associates, Inc.


Ethan J. Wagner
Charles Winner

Paul Mandabach
Cristina Rose
Bob Garcia
Scott Fitz-Randolph
Richard Lichtenstein
Charles S. Warn
Sande George
Jeanette Fruen Turk
Buff McDonnell
Don Whitehead
Steven D. Burton
Ralph F. Simoni
Terrance W. Flanigan
Ross S. Clark

March 26, 1981


Mr. Rich Eichman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Eichman:

Pursuant to our conversation last week I am writing to ask whether
our client, the Coalition of Apparel Industries in California,
must report receipts to their operating fund (either pro-rata or in full)
if monies from that operating fund have been used for campaign contributions.
There were two occasions upon which our client used monies from their
operating fund to make campaign contributions; one occasion occurred on
June 15, 1979, to ten legislators for a total of $4,850. and the other
was on November 19, 1980, to one legislator for $2,500.

The Coalition has been registered as a Recipient Committee at least
since January 1975. As a matter of course, the Coalition, separate from
their operating fund, has solicited contributions from its members when
it anticipates making political contributions. With reference to the
two occasions above it is my understanding that the monies which comprised
the operating fund had no stipulation placed on them as to how they were
to be used. In other words, the members contributing to the Coalition's
operating fund did not necessarily have reason to know those monies
would be used for campaign contributions.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. If you need further
information to determine your decision, please contact me.

Sincerely,

Laura Seidenberg
Office Manager

cc: Dick Blumenthal
Marge Carne
1024 Tenth Street, Sacramento, California 95814 · (916) 444-9333
6535 Wilshire Blvd., Los Angeles, California 90048 · (213) 653-1130

sections.question: Your question is whether the receipts of the operating fund of the Coalition of Apparel Industries must be reported since campaign contributions of $4,850 and $2,500 were made from that fund in 1979 and 1980 respectively.
sections.conclusion: Upon review of the facts presented in your letter, the staff has concluded that the receipts of the operating fund must be reported for 1980. Because contributions were made from the fund in 1979, members contributing to the fund in 1980 have reason to know that a portion of these funds may be used for political purposes. To avoid reporting receipts of the operating funds in the future the guidelines as set forth in 2 Cal. Admin. Code 18419 (copy attached) should be followed.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Coalition of Apparel Industries must report receipts from its operating fund when campaign contributions were made from that fund in prior years, specifically when members contributing to the fund had no stipulation that funds would be used for political purposes.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that the Coalition must report operating fund receipts for 1980 because members contributing in 1980 would have reason to know funds could be used for political purposes, given the 1979 contributions. Future reporting can be avoided by following guidelines in 2 Cal. Admin. Code 18419.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (March 26, 1981) and the FPPC response letter (April 14, 1981). Both Q and C sections clearly identified. High confidence extraction based on explicit question and conclusion statements.

CONCLUSION: Upon review of the facts presented in your letter, the staff has concluded that the receipts of the operating fund must be reported for 1980. Because contributions were made from the fund in 1979, members contributing to the fund in 1980 have reason to know that a portion of these funds may be used for political purposes. To avoid reporting receipts of the operating funds in the future the guidelines as set forth in 2 Cal. Admin. Code 18419 (copy attached) should be followed.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter responding to Winner/Wagner & Associates' inquiry on behalf of the Coalition of Apparel Industries regarding whether operating fund receipts must be reported when campaign contributions were made from that fund. The FPPC concludes that receipts must be reported for 1980 and recommends following specific administrative code guidelines to avoid future reporting requirements.
